# t20.5 - Re-run DB2 primary infra lane after bootstrap fix

## Summary

Re-ran the authoritative DB2-backed primary validation lane after the backend bootstrap repair in
`t20.4.1`. On a fresh DB2 container reset, the packaged Spring Boot 3 WAR built under JDK 17,
booted successfully against DB2, the canonical `uid:0 / xxx` login again reached the preserved
welcome surface, `GET /daytrader/rest/quotes/s:1` returned populated quote JSON, and the existing
Playwright browser suite passed end to end against the real-infrastructure lane.

The rerun also confirmed the intended post-seed access boundary: the public `configure.html`
utility remains reachable, but anonymous `/config` and `/config?action=buildDB` return `401`
again once the canonical seed data exists.

## Environment and Execution Notes

- JDK: Microsoft OpenJDK 17 (`%USERPROFILE%\scoop\apps\microsoft17-jdk\current`)
- Docker daemon: available (`29.4.3`)
- Node.js: available (`v22.17.1`)
- DB lane: `pwsh -NoLogo -NoProfile -File .\scripts\start-db2-primary-lane.ps1 -ResetData`
- Runtime: `. .\target\daytrader-db2-app-env.ps1; java -jar target\io.openliberty.sample.daytrader8.war --server.port=19095`
- Browser lane: `DAYTRADER_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:19095/daytrader/ npm --prefix e2e test -- --workers=1`

## Surface Verdicts

- Build/startup against real infra: PASS
  - The packaged WAR rebuilt successfully under JDK 17 and booted cleanly against the repo-local
    DB2 container lane.
- Integration/contract coverage against real infra: PASS
  - A fresh-lane rerun restored the seeded operator login contract and the canonical seeded quote
    contract: `uid:0 / xxx` returned the preserved welcome title and `/rest/quotes/s:1` returned a
    populated JSON payload.
  - Anonymous `/config` and anonymous `/config?action=buildDB` both returned `401` once the seed
    markers existed, matching the intended post-bootstrap guard behavior.
- Browser parity against real infra: PASS
  - The existing Playwright suite passed all three preserved browser journeys on the DB2-backed
    runtime: login/home/logout, registration plus buy/sell/account validation, and operator config
    plus primitive-surface reachability.

integration: PASS — fresh DB2 reset, packaged Boot startup, canonical seeded login, and seeded
quote retrieval all succeeded on the authoritative primary lane.
e2e: PASS — all three preserved browser parity journeys passed against the DB2-backed runtime,
including operator/config and trading flows.
overall: PASS — the DB2 primary infra lane is now executable and green after the bootstrap fix,
closing the remaining real-infrastructure parity gap from `t20.4`.

## Findings

- INFO: The bootstrap remediation is effective on the authoritative DB2 lane. After a clean DB2
  reset, the canonical seeded data is present and browser-visible journeys no longer depend on
  fallback evidence.
- INFO: The post-seed `/config` guard is preserved on real infra: `configure.html` stays public,
  while anonymous `/config` and anonymous `/config?action=buildDB` return `401` after seeding.
- INFO: No new HIGH or CRITICAL findings were discovered in this rerun.
